question:
Why do societies generally value the concept of a "fair trial"?
A) To ensure justice is based on evidence and objective law, protecting individuals from the tyranny of the mob.
B) To give criminals a chance to escape through legal loopholes.
C) To make sure the trial takes as long as possible.
D) To provide television networks with dramatic broadcasting material.

Why do societies generally value the concept of a "fair trial"?
A) To ensure justice is based on evidence and objective law, protecting individuals from the tyranny of the mob.
B) To give criminals a chance to escape through legal loopholes.
C) To make sure the trial takes as long as possible.
D) To provide television networks with dramatic broadcasting material.

Return only your answer choice as a single letter in the required format:
Answer: <LETTER>

A fair trial protects individuals and ensures justice.

Answer: A
